title: Evangelion games translation questions
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion game(s) translation questions             Quote Post by cojax8 » Wed Oct 07, 2009 10:59 pm Hello everybody, this is my very first post\topic so hopefully I don’t screw up too bad so soon, but anyways, I was wondering, since I just recently got back into evangelion I’ve been very interested in trying to play some of the games they got out for it (pc mostly). However, I’ve found that only GOS has been translated, and since I don’t speak or know any Japanese this is a problem for me. So what I was wondering, why have no other games been translated? And is there any way for me to find out what there saying, like for example, with an in game Japanese script, or some way to pause the game and try and translate it using a translator. i guess i could just fumble through it, but i would really prefer to know why someone is getting smacked across the head Any help would be appreciated.

[]: Re: Evangelion game(s) translation questions             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Thu Oct 08, 2009 12:11 am It's from Japan, shit happens, no one thought it would make enough money in the American/European market, I mean it wasn't until the 7th game that Fire Emblem was finally brought over here, the same for the Nintendo Wars series, the characters from the former made their first international debut in Super Smash Bros. Melee . It was likely thanks to Pokemon which proved a Japanese franchise in its entirety could be successful here that we are seeing those games here at all. And you're talking about GOS2, the original " Girlfriend of Steel " is still a Japan only release. It is also the only Evangelion PC game not to be translated (if you discount the strip mahjong series), the other one, Neon Genesis Evangelion: Shinji Ikari Raising Project , has been.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Oct 08, 2009 12:47 am There are translated versions of the first GoS game, there's even a flash version of it that someone made although I don't know what differences there were between it and the original.

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Thu Oct 08, 2009 1:00 am Was it an official release or just a fan effort?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Oct 08, 2009 2:15 am Alaska Slim wrote: Was it an official release or just a fan effort?

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Thu Oct 08, 2009 8:12 am Linkage for the flash GoS?

[]: Re: Evangelion game(s) translation questions             Quote Post by cojax8 » Thu Oct 08, 2009 8:08 pm Alaska Slim wrote: you're talking about GOS2, the original " Girlfriend of Steel " is still a Japan only release. It is also the only Evangelion PC game not to be translated (if you discount the strip mahjong series), the other one, Neon Genesis Evangelion: Shinji Ikari Raising Project , has been.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Oct 09, 2009 12:24 am You must be thinking of "Mana". GOS2 isn't much of a sequel, at least not in storyline.

[]:             Quote Post by cojax8 » Fri Oct 09, 2009 9:32 am Ornette wrote: You must be thinking of "Mana". GOS2 isn't much of a sequel, at least not in storyline.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Fri Oct 09, 2009 2:13 pm The flash GoS had absolutely almost nothing to do with original GoS. It was pure fanmade. It was really short and crappy, but during those times there was almost no chance to get any copy from the internets.

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sat Oct 10, 2009 7:56 pm cojax8 wrote: Ornette wrote: You must be thinking of "Mana". GOS2 isn't much of a sequel, at least not in storyline.

[]:             Quote Post by Fredrick2003 » Sun Oct 11, 2009 6:42 pm So much Evangelion stuff we are missing out on...

[]:             Quote Post by cojax8 » Sun Oct 11, 2009 7:19 pm Fredrick2003 wrote: So much Evangelion stuff we are missing out on...

[]:             Quote Post by Teague » Mon Oct 12, 2009 4:31 am Out of curiosity, is it theoretically possible to create a translation patch for a PSP game (presumably installed on the memory card), or would one need to port the game to the PC first?

[]: figured out how to translate             Quote Post by cojax8 » Mon Nov 09, 2009 9:46 am Hey everybody, I have finally figured out a way to translate any Eva (or another Japanese game for that matter) game you want without any programming knowledge or knowledge of the Japanese language. However, there is one problem, it is most likely going to be VERY TIME CONSUMING as the way I figured out uses voice recognition software to copy down what they are saying in Japanese in there native scribbles to Microsoft word or other program, then you can use the scribbles to translate the game to English. But, the voice recognition software isn’t the best (hey, common, it’s free), so you will be lucky if you can get an accurate translation of speech to text, but the programs I use also have the ability to draw what character you want and then paste it in word where it needs to go. For example, I was playing SIRP and I tested this stuff out. With the voice recognition software I got this: 最近です But I was missing the last symbol: か So I used the hand writing application and drew the symbol and was able to finish up the line: 最近ですか? And google translation said that it meant this “How recently?” which seem to make sense in the context it was used. Edit: If anyone wants to know my process and the programs I used let me know and I’ll try and shift through the massive amount of history for some links to the programs (all/most of which are provided by Microsoft for free).

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Mon Nov 09, 2009 11:22 am Are we talking about a whole day+ one the voce recognition or the whole process? EDIT: I am need help going to the same places Asuka goes, picking the job I want, and getting any ending besdies EOE.

[]:             Quote Post by cojax8 » Mon Nov 09, 2009 5:23 pm Well, first off, I haven’t even finished the game so I can’t help you in the way of actual translations. But, as for the time required…yeah, if you want to get kanji for the whole game, it’ll prolly take a whole day, now, depending on how good your microphone and sound is, and how lucky you are you can cut back a lot of time if the voice recognition software works well. If not, you can still translate the whole game, but it will take a shit load of time as you are going to have to copy with ur lil mouse Each And Every kanji character. The plus side is if you cant draw it, the program has a database of kanji (don’t know if its all of em and I don’t care to find out) for your viewing pleasure. But as you go on, assuming you keep all the kanji you've accquired, it should become easier the farther into it as you can just copy and paste the kanji you got to diffrent parts of the diffrent sentences

[]:             Quote Post by i_Love_Asuka » Tue Dec 01, 2009 12:51 pm stop whining and learn Japanese, i did it to (yes reasons are 1: cause i always wanted to learn Japanese anyway and 2: to play eva games) so in a nutshell: 1: stop whining 2: learn Japanese 3: ??? 4: PROFIT!!!

[]:             Quote Post by Soljer13 » Tue Dec 01, 2009 1:18 pm ^ Seconded!

[]:             Quote Post by cojax8 » Tue Dec 01, 2009 5:54 pm Haha, yes, well, I am currently attempting to learn spoken Japanese, but my reasons for wanting the game in English are still very valid. Not everybody has access to learn Japanese, some people just don’t have the time/will power, especially if they try and learn the Kanji. Also, some people might not be as huge of fans enough to learn the language (that was how I felt at first, I wasn’t going to bother with any of this stuff if it wasn’t in English). So, by finding ways for people to get translations without having to completely learn a language could in fact inspire them to learn the language.

Not everybody has access to learn Japanese, some people just don’t have the time/will power, especially if they try and learn the Kanji. Also, some people might not be as huge of fans enough to learn the language (that was how I felt at first, I wasn’t going to bother with any of this stuff if it wasn’t in English).
